chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P.NO.S-1229 OF 2017 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on MA-6296/2017 2. For orders on office objection. 3. For orders on MA-2636/2017 4. For katcha peshi. 02.10.2017 None present for the petitioner. However, since the life and liberty of the petitioner is involved in this petitioner, therefore, this matter is taken up. Mr. Imtiaz Ali Mahar, State Counsel. = 1. Granted. 2. Over-ruled 3. Granted subject to all just exceptions. 4. With reference to the notice of previous date, learned State Counsel files statement/parawise comments on behalf of respondents No.2, 3 and 4, which are taken on record. According to the parawise comments, the controversy between the petitioner and the private respondents No.5 to 9 appears to be over matrimonial relation which is a civil nature dispute. The police officials in their comments have also stated that neither the petitioner has approached to the concerned police station for redressal of his grievances nor he has been harassed, therefore, the question of harassment on behalf of the police does not arise, however, the official respondents have undertaken that the petitioner will be provided legal protection in accordance with law, in the event if the petitioner approaches them with such a request. Be that as it may, this petition is being disposed of with direction to the private respondents No.5 to 9 namely Abdul Haq, Ghulam Nabi, Ghulam Sarwar, Abdul Rasool and Ghulam Rasool, all by caste Mengal, not to cause any harassment to the petitioner with further direction to the official respondents to discharge their functions and duties strictly in accordance with law in a fair and reasonable manner without patronizing any group or individuals.

The police officials in their comments have also stated that neither the petitioner has approached to the concerned police station for redressal of his grievances nor he has been harassed, therefore, the question of harassment on behalf of the police does not arise, however, the official respondents have undertaken that the petitioner will be provided legal protection in accordance with law, in the event if the petitioner approaches them with such a request. Be that as it may, this petition is being disposed of with direction to the private respondents No.5 to 9 namely Abdul Haq, Ghulam Nabi, Ghulam Sarwar, Abdul Rasool and Ghulam Rasool, all by caste Mengal, not to cause any harassment to the petitioner with further direction to the official respondents to discharge their functions and duties strictly in accordance with law in a fair and reasonable manner without patronizing any group or individuals. The petitioner and the private respondents are at liberty to seek remedy of their grievances, if any, before the appropriate forum by invoking due process of law and not by taking law in their hands. Since the private respondents No.5 to 9 despite notice are not in attendance, therefore, the concerned SHO is also directed to serve the copy of this order on the above named private respondents by calling them in Police Station and compliance of such service should be furnished with the Additional Registrar of this Court. JUDGE Shahid
